explicit_text: I experienced the Sylmar earthquake back in 1971, and it was a frightening event. I was living at home with my parents in Van Nuys at the time, and the quake woke me up at 6:01 a.m. It was the worst earthquake we had experienced up to that point, and we were scared by the shaking and the aftershocks that followed. That earthquake was when we first learned about aftershocks.

explicit_text: The 1994 Northridge earthquake was a terrifying experience for us. We were asleep at 4:30 a.m. with guests in the house when the shaking knocked us out of bed. Our home was damaged with broken glass, fallen furniture, and the power went out, but thankfully we had emergency lights that helped us navigate. We evacuated safely and saw fires on the hills north of Northridge. It was a chaotic and scary time, but we were grateful to be safe.
